sectionOrderRules: Hero must state the business type within fiske eller vattenbruk, and include a primary contact CTA.
Business summary should explain what the company does in concrete terms such as fiske, odling, leverans, förädling or försäljning.
Products-services-summary must highlight actual offerings without implying stock or species that are not provided in the source data.
Trust-proof must appear before the final contact CTA and should use real signals such as organisationsnummer, referenser, leveransområde or transparent working methods.
Opening-hours and map-preview should only be shown when the business has visitor-relevant times or a physical location.
Services intro must connect the business identity to the actual offering, for example försäljning av fisk och skaldjur, vattenbruk, leverans, rådgivning or entreprenadtjänster.
Service-list items must be concrete and specific, not generic promises.
Pricing-table may only be used when prices, examples or ordering formats are actually provided.
Contact CTA should invite enquiry about availability, beställning or samarbete without promising delivery capacity that is not confirmed.
Sustainability intro must describe the company's real arbetssätt, handling, odlingsmiljö or quality routines without claiming certifications unless supplied.
Work-process should explain how products or services are handled from fångst eller odling to leverans, with plain language.
Quality-trust may include traceability, hygiene routines, local presence or references, but must avoid invented labels or guarantees.
This page should educate and reassure, not repeat the same service copy as the products-services page.
About story must be specific to the business history, place, ownership or daily work, not generic brand language.
Team should only show named people or clear role descriptions when such data exists.
Trust-proof should reinforce reliability with real business facts and references without duplicating every item already shown elsewhere.
Contact info must include real phone, email and address or service location details when available.
Contact-form should offer a simple path for order enquiries, partnership questions or general contact.
Map-embed should only be shown when a real visitable or pickup-relevant location exists.
Opening-hours should only be shown when they are relevant for customers or visitors.
